title: hieracium glanduliferum subsp. subnivale
intro: Hieracium glanduliferum subsp. subnivale, also known as the Alpine Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that grows in high-altitude mountainous regions. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. The health benefits associated with Hieracium glanduliferum subsp. subnivale include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
